dialogue_line_replacements.text: ¿Qué tú hiciste el fin de semana pasado?
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: What did you do last weekend? (PR subject pronoun order)

dialogue_line_replacements.text: ¡Qué bien! Yo salí con unas amiguitas el sábado.
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: How nice! I went out with some girlfriends on Saturday.

cultural_notes_overrides.formality: In Puerto Rico, the preterite is used the same way as in Mexico. A distinctive feature: subject pronouns are placed before the verb more often — '¿Qué tú hiciste?' instead of '¿Qué hiciste tú?'
cultural_notes_overrides.gestures: Puerto Ricans are expressive storytellers — hand gestures and facial expressions accompany past-tense narratives naturally.
cultural_notes_overrides.regional_variations: Puerto Rican Spanish frequently uses the present perfect where Mexico uses the preterite: 'He comido' vs 'Comí'. Both are understood but PR speakers lean toward the compound form in some contexts.
